positions.detail: Down -100% — stop loss triggered at -50%. Close position.

agent_analysis:
## POSITION MONITOR — Thursday (ELEVATED)

---

### AAPL CALL — **CLOSED / STOP HIT**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Stock move | **-5.6%** |
| Option P&L | **-100%** (total loss) |
| Status | STOP_HIT |
| Thursday target | 15% | n/a — position is dead |

A -5.6% move against a weekly call is catastrophic. At 0.35 entry delta, the option went deep OTM; charm + theta collapsed whatever residual value remained after the directional hit. The -50% hard stop should have triggered earlier in the week — the fact that P&L reads -100% means either the stop wasn't executed or the gap was too fast for orderly exit. This needs a process review in Sunday's reflection.

**Ensemble check:** Whatever directional signals supported this call — momentum, trend persistence — were definitively wrong. A 5.6% weekly decline is a ~2σ+ move for AAPL. Check whether this was earnings/news-driven or a broad market event.

---

### NO OTHER POSITIONS REPORTED

Only one position provided. If the other two picks were entered, their status is needed. If they were skipped at Monday entry (gap stop, delta drift), confirm that explicitly.

---

## RECOMMENDATION

**Close AAPL immediately if any residual value exists. Accept the total loss.**

At -100% there is nothing to manage. If the option still shows a bid (even $0.01-0.05), sell it now — do not hold into Friday hoping for a miracle reversal on a deep OTM weekly with one day left. Theta will consume any remaining premium overnight.

**Action items for reflection:**
1. The -50% hard stop should cap losses at ~$200 on a typical position, not -100%. Investigate whether the stop was set but gapped through, or never triggered.
2. AAPL is now 4 losses in a row in the trade log (3 prior CALL losses on other tickers this cycle). The system's CALL bias is 0/10 over 8 weeks. This is a structural problem, not bad luck. Sunday reflection must address whether the direction model is systematically miscalibrating bullish signals.
